markdown.markdown:
## A framework for data visualization {.smaller}

Introduced by Leland Wilkinson ([The Grammar of Graphics](https://link.springer.com/book/10.1007/0-387-28695-0 "Access provided by U. Bordeaux"), 2005).

Graphics can be built up from a set of components:

::::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
-   [Data]{.fg style="--col: #184669"}: the dataset to be visualized
-   [Variables]{.fg style="--col: #184669"}: mapping of objects to values
-   [Algebra]{.fg style="--col: #184669"}: operations to combine variables and specify dimensions
-   [Scales]{.fg style="--col: #184669"}: represent variables on measured dimensions
-   [Statistics]{.fg style="--col: #184669"}: functions to change the appearance and representation
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
-   [Geometry]{.fg style="--col: #184669"}: creation of geometric graphs from variables
-   [Coordinates]{.fg style="--col: #184669"}: mapping to coordinate systems
-   [Aesthetics]{.fg style="--col: #184669"}: sensory attributes
-   [Facets]{.fg style="--col: #184669"}: subplots based on subsets of data
-   [Guides]{.fg style="--col: #184669"}: legends and axes to explain the graph
:::
:::::

## Adapted to R in the `ggplot2` package 

![](resources/overview_graphic-1.png)

## Example: Data 

```{r}
#| label: data
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns3070
library(dplyr)
library(data.table)
library(DT)
library(ggplot2)

DT::datatable(mpg)
```
## Example: Data {auto-animate=TRUE}

```{r}
#| label: ggplot_data 
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns4060
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-asp: 1
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(mpg)
```

## Example: Mapping {auto-animate=TRUE}

```{r}
#| label: ggplot_mapping 
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns4060
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-asp: 1
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(
  mpg,
  mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy)
)
```

## Example: Layers {auto-animate=TRUE}
```{r}
#| label: ggplot_layers 
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns4060
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-asp: 1
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(
  mpg,
  mapping = aes(
    x = cty,
    y = hwy
  )
) +
  geom_point()
```



## Example: Scales {auto-animate=TRUE}
```{r}
#| label: ggplot_scales
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns4060
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-asp: 1
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(
  mpg,
  mapping = aes(
    x = displ,
    y = hwy,
    colour = class
  )
) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_colour_viridis_d()
```

## Example: Facets {auto-animate=TRUE}
```{r}
#| label: ggplot_facets
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns4060
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-asp: 1
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(
  mpg,
  mapping = aes(
    x = displ,
    y = hwy,
    colour = class
  )
) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_colour_viridis_d() +
  facet_grid(year ~ class)
```

## Example: Coordinates {auto-animate=TRUE}
```{r}
#| label: ggplot_coordinates
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns4060
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-asp: 1
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(
  mpg,
  mapping = aes(
    x = displ,
    y = hwy,
    colour = class
  )
) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_colour_viridis_d() +
  facet_grid(year ~ class) +
  coord_cartesian(
    xlim = c(0, 8),
    ylim = c(10, 50)
  )
```

## Example: Theme {auto-animate=TRUE .code5}
```{r}
#| label: ggplot_theme
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns4060
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-asp: 1
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(
  mpg,
  mapping = aes(
    x = displ,
    y = hwy,
    colour = class
  )
) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_colour_viridis_d() +
  facet_grid(year ~ class) +
  coord_cartesian(
    xlim = c(0, 8),
    ylim = c(10, 50),
    clip = "off"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    axis.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
    axis.line = element_line(color = "black", size = 0.75),
    axis.ticks = element_line(color = "black", size = 0.75),
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10)
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    name = "Engine Displacement (L)",
    breaks = seq(0, 8, 4),
    limits = c(0, 8),
    expand = c(0, 0)
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(name = "Highway Miles per Gallon") +
  labs(colour = "Vehicle Class")
```

# Reminders on variables, data types and basic operations {background-color="#184669"}

## Tidyverse install verification {.out7 .code7} 

```{r}
#| label: check_environment
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

if (!requireNamespace("tidyverse", quietly = TRUE)) {
  install.packages("tidyverse")
} 

library(tidyverse)
```

## Data.table install verification {.out7 .code7} 

```{r}
#| label: check_environment_2
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

if (!requireNamespace("data.table", quietly = TRUE)) {
  install.packages("data.table")
} 

library(data.table)

# version check
packageVersion("data.table")
```

```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_1

#| classes: columns5050

a <- 42

a

```

```{r}
a_string <- "fourty-two"

a_string
```

## Variables: booleans {auto-animate=TRUE}

```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_2prime
#| echo: true
#| eval: true

#| message: true

c(a_boolean, b_boolean)
```

## Missing values and infinities {auto-animate=TRUE}

```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_2second
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

a_missing <- NA
a_nan <- NaN
a_inf <- Inf
b_inf <- -Inf
char_missing <- NA_character_
real_missing <- NA_real_
int_missing <- NA_integer_

tb_1 <- tibble(
  missing = a_missing,
  not_a_number = a_nan,
  infinite = a_inf,
  negative_infinite = b_inf,
  char_missing = char_missing,
  real_missing = real_missing,
  int_missing = int_missing
)

tb_1
glimpse(tb_1)
```

## Missing values and infinities {auto-animate=TRUE}

```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_2second_2
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

dt_1 <- data.table(
  missing = a_missing,
  not_a_number = a_nan,
  infinite = a_inf,
  negative_infinite = b_inf,
  char_missing = char_missing,
  real_missing = real_missing,
  int_missing = int_missing
)

dt_1
```

```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_2second_2p
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

str(dt_1)
```

## Vectors {auto-animate=TRUE .out7}

```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_3
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

a_vector <- c(1:10, "coucou")

a_vector

```


## Vector subsetting {auto-animate=TRUE}

```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_3second
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

a_vector <- c(1:10, "coucou")
# R is 1-indexed
# so the first element is at position 1
a_vector[c(1:3,11)]

```

## Vector subsetting by many other ways {auto-animate=TRUE}

```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_3third
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

a_vector <- c(1:10, "coucou")

tibble(
  'index' = a_vector[c(1:3, 11)],
  'logical' = a_vector[
    c(TRUE, TRUE, TRUE, rep(FALSE, 7), TRUE)
  ],
  'negative' = a_vector[-c(4:10)]
)
```

Why are all values characters?
We will see that in a minute!

## Naming elements of a vector {auto-animate=TRUE}

```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_3fourth
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

b_vector <- c(1:5)

names(b_vector) <- c(
  "one", "two", "three", "four", "five"
  )

b_vector
```


## Accessing named elements of a vector {auto-animate=TRUE}

```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_3fifth
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

b_vector["three"] #named element
```


```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_3sixth
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

b_vector[["three"]] #scalar element
```

## Lists {auto-animate=TRUE}

```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_4
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

# lists cannot contain different types of data
# but  can contain different types of objects, 
# including vectors
a_list <- list(
  numeric_vector = c(1, 2, 3, 4),
  character_vector = c("a", "b", "c"),
  mixed_vector = c(1, "b", TRUE),
  nested_list = list(
    numeric_vector = c(5, 6, 7, 8),
    character_vector = c("d", "e", "f"),
    mixed_vector = c(9, "g", FALSE)
  )
)

a_list
```

## List item access by name {auto-animate=TRUE} 
```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_5
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050
#| code-line-numbers: "4,13|4,13,7|4,13,7,15"

# lists cannot contain different types of data
# but  can contain different types of objects, 
# including vectors
a_list <- list(
  numeric_vector = c(1, 2, 3, 4),
  character_vector = c("a", "b", "c"),
  mixed_vector = c(1, "b", TRUE),
  nested_list = list(
    numeric_vector = c(5, 6, 7, 8),
    character_vector = c("d", "e", "f"),
    mixed_vector = c(9, "g", FALSE)
  )
)

a_list$mixed_vector
```

## List item access by name {auto-animate=TRUE} 
```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_6
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050
#| code-line-numbers: "4,13|4,13,8,12|4,13,8,12,10|4,13,8,12,10,15"

# lists cannot contain different types of data
# but  can contain different types of objects, 
# including vectors
a_list <- list(
  numeric_vector = c(1, 2, 3, 4),
  character_vector = c("a", "b", "c"),
  mixed_vector = c(1, "b", TRUE),
  nested_list = list(
    numeric_vector = c(5, 6, 7, 8),
    character_vector = c("d", "e", "f"),
    mixed_vector = c(9, "g", FALSE)
  )
)

a_list$nested_list$character_vector
```

## List item access by many other ways {auto-animate=TRUE .out7} 
```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_7
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

# lists cannot contain different types of data
# but  can contain different types of objects,
# including vectors
a_list <- list(
  numeric_vector = c(1, 2, 3, 4),
  character_vector = c("a", "b", "c"),
  mixed_vector = c(1, "b", TRUE),
  nested_list = list(
    numeric_vector = c(5, 6, 7, 8),
    character_vector = c("d", "e", "f"),
    mixed_vector = c(9, "g", FALSE)
  )
)

tibble(
  'dollar_name' = a_list$character_vector,
  'bracket_name' = a_list[["character_vector"]],
  'bracket_index' = a_list[[2]],
  'bracket_twice' = a_list[2][[1]]
)
```

what happens if we access `a_list[2]`?

## Data frames {auto-animate=TRUE}

```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_10
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

# Data frame = named list of vectors
a_df <- data.frame(
  id = 1:5,
  group = c("A", "A", "B", "B", "C"),
  value = c(10.2, 12.5, 9.8, 11.1, 13.0)
)

a_df
```


## Basic functions {auto-animate=TRUE}

```{r}
#| label: base_r_f_1
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

a <- 1
b <- pi
c <- a + b
print(
  paste(
    "The result of a + b is:",
    c
  )
)
```

## Basic functions {auto-animate=TRUE}

```{r}
#| label: base_f_2
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050
#| code-line-numbers: "3|4,9"

a <- 1
b <- 2
c <- sum(a, b)
cat(
  paste(
    "The result of a + b is:",
    c
  )
)
```

## Basic functions {auto-animate=TRUE}

```{r}
#| label: base_r_5
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050
#| code-line-numbers: "3"

a <- 1
b <- 2
c <- mean(c(a, b))
cat(
  paste(
    "The mean of a and b is:",
    c
  )
)
```


# Data preparation {background-color="#184669"}

## Starting dataset {auto-animate=TRUE .out7}
```{r}
#| label: data_preparation_1
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns4060
library(ggplot2)

head(mpg)
```

## Use of glimpse {auto-animate=TRUE .out7}
```{r}
#| label: data_preparation_2
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns4060
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

glimpse(mpg)
```

## Use of select  {auto-animate=TRUE .out7}
```{r}
#| label: data_preparation_3
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns4060
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

mpg %>%
  select(displ, hwy, class, year) |> 
  glimpse()


```

# Introduction to Grammar of Graphics {background-color="#184669"}

## A framework for data visualization {.smaller}

Introduced by Leland Wilkinson ([The Grammar of Graphics](https://link.springer.com/book/10.1007/0-387-28695-0 "Access provided by U. Bordeaux"), 2005).

Graphics can be built up from a set of components:

::::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
-   [Data]{.fg style="--col: #184669"}: the dataset to be visualized
-   [Variables]{.fg style="--col: #184669"}: mapping of objects to values
-   [Algebra]{.fg style="--col: #184669"}: operations to combine variables and specify dimensions
-   [Scales]{.fg style="--col: #184669"}: represent variables on measured dimensions
-   [Statistics]{.fg style="--col: #184669"}: functions to change the appearance and representation
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
-   [Geometry]{.fg style="--col: #184669"}: creation of geometric graphs from variables
-   [Coordinates]{.fg style="--col: #184669"}: mapping to coordinate systems
-   [Aesthetics]{.fg style="--col: #184669"}: sensory attributes
-   [Facets]{.fg style="--col: #184669"}: subplots based on subsets of data
-   [Guides]{.fg style="--col: #184669"}: legends and axes to explain the graph
:::
:::::

## Adapted to R in the `ggplot2` package 

![](resources/overview_graphic-1.png)

## Example: Data 

```{r}
#| label: data
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns3070
library(dplyr)
library(data.table)
library(DT)
library(ggplot2)

DT::datatable(mpg)
```

## Example: Theme {auto-animate=TRUE .code5}
```{r}
#| label: ggplot_theme
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns4060
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-asp: 1
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(
  mpg,
  mapping = aes(
    x = displ,
    y = hwy,
    colour = class
  )
) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_colour_viridis_d() +
  facet_grid(year ~ class) +
  coord_cartesian(
    xlim = c(0, 8),
    ylim = c(10, 50),
    clip = "off"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    axis.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
    axis.line = element_line(color = "black", size = 0.75),
    axis.ticks = element_line(color = "black", size = 0.75),
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10)
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    name = "Engine Displacement (L)",
    breaks = seq(0, 8, 4),
    limits = c(0, 8),
    expand = c(0, 0)
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(name = "Highway Miles per Gallon") +
  labs(colour = "Vehicle Class")
```

## Missing values and infinities {auto-animate=TRUE}

```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_2second
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

a_missing <- NA
a_nan <- NaN
a_inf <- Inf
b_inf <- -Inf
char_missing <- NA_character_
real_missing <- NA_real_
int_missing <- NA_integer_

tb_1 <- tibble(
  missing = a_missing,
  not_a_number = a_nan,
  infinite = a_inf,
  negative_infinite = b_inf,
  char_missing = char_missing,
  real_missing = real_missing,
  int_missing = int_missing
)

tb_1
glimpse(tb_1)
```

```{r}
a_vector
```

## Vector subsetting {auto-animate=TRUE}

```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_3second
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

a_vector <- c(1:10, "coucou")
# R is 1-indexed
# so the first element is at position 1
a_vector[c(1:3,11)]

```

## Vector subsetting by many other ways {auto-animate=TRUE}

```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_3third
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

a_vector <- c(1:10, "coucou")

tibble(
  'index' = a_vector[c(1:3, 11)],
  'logical' = a_vector[
    c(TRUE, TRUE, TRUE, rep(FALSE, 7), TRUE)
  ],
  'negative' = a_vector[-c(4:10)]
)
```

Why are all values characters?
We will see that in a minute!

## Lists {auto-animate=TRUE}

```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_4
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

# lists cannot contain different types of data
# but  can contain different types of objects, 
# including vectors
a_list <- list(
  numeric_vector = c(1, 2, 3, 4),
  character_vector = c("a", "b", "c"),
  mixed_vector = c(1, "b", TRUE),
  nested_list = list(
    numeric_vector = c(5, 6, 7, 8),
    character_vector = c("d", "e", "f"),
    mixed_vector = c(9, "g", FALSE)
  )
)

a_list
```

## List item access by many other ways {auto-animate=TRUE .out7} 
```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_7
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

# lists cannot contain different types of data
# but  can contain different types of objects,
# including vectors
a_list <- list(
  numeric_vector = c(1, 2, 3, 4),
  character_vector = c("a", "b", "c"),
  mixed_vector = c(1, "b", TRUE),
  nested_list = list(
    numeric_vector = c(5, 6, 7, 8),
    character_vector = c("d", "e", "f"),
    mixed_vector = c(9, "g", FALSE)
  )
)

tibble(
  'dollar_name' = a_list$character_vector,
  'bracket_name' = a_list[["character_vector"]],
  'bracket_index' = a_list[[2]],
  'bracket_twice' = a_list[2][[1]]
)
```

what happens if we access `a_list[2]`?

## Basic functions {auto-animate=TRUE}
